surface.name: Serverless Quickstart, cURL chat completions
surface.selection_basis: Fireworks' Serverless Quickstart is the documented fastest start. It includes a cURL tab that POSTs to https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1/chat/completions with Authorization: Bearer $FIREWORKS_API_KEY, so a Python SDK install is not required. Prepaid credits and a payment method remain required before usage.

alternatives_considered.name: Fireworks Python SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Optional client library; the same quickstart documents an equivalent cURL request.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenAI-compatible Python SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Third-party SDK convenience route to the same HTTP endpoint.

alternatives_considered.name: Anthropic-compatible SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Compatibility tab, not required for the first Serverless call.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Fireworks account, fund prepaid credits, obtain an API key, and complete a Serverless chat-completions request over HTTPS.
assumed_prior_state: No Fireworks account, payment method, credits, API key, or prior request is assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Make your first Serverless API call
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated cURL POST to Fireworks Serverless chat completions returns model output such as a Spanish greeting.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The HTTP response body includes assistant content like `¡Hola!`.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: You should see a response like: `¡Hola!`

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart demonstrates this as the first Serverless API call. cURL is documented alongside SDKs, so installing fireworks-ai is optional.

prerequisites.requirement: An email address or Google, GitHub, or LinkedIn identity for one of the current signup routes.
Signup signifies agreement to Fireworks Terms of Service and Data Processing Agreement.
A valid payment method and billing address, followed by prepaid credits before usage according to the current credit-system guide.
A Fireworks API key stored safely and exported locally.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client). No language SDK is required.

primary_path.action: Open the Serverless Quickstart and follow its Fireworks dashboard key link or account entry.
primary_path.output: The Fireworks account or dashboard route opens.
primary_path.success_signal: A new user can reach Create Account.

primary_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to Fireworks.
details: Treat signup as one opaque workshop-policy step at the official URL.
Signup signifies agreement to the Terms of Service and Data Processing Agreement.
primary_path.success_signal: User Settings and Billing are accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed account recovery path.

primary_path.action: Ensure the account has usable prepaid credits.
details: Add the documented payment method and billing address and purchase credits when the account has no usable balance.
primary_path.success_signal: The account is not paused for zero credits.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Resolve payment verification, budget, or missing-credit issues using the billing guidance.

primary_path.action: Create and copy an API key.
details: Open Profile, User Settings, API Keys, then select Create API Key.
primary_path.success_signal: The new key is displayed and copied.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Verify account access and key permissions.

primary_path.output: Credential available to the SDK process.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct shell quoting or environment configuration.

primary_path.action: POST https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1/chat/completions with cURL using the Bearer API key.
details: Include Content-Type: application/json and Authorization: Bearer $FIREWORKS_API_KEY.
Use model accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v3p1 and message Say hello in Spanish as in the quickstart cURL example.
primary_path.input: Exported FIREWORKS_API_KEY and usable prepaid credits
primary_path.success_signal: The response includes model output such as ¡Hola!.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check API key, credit balance, model id, and network errors.

branches.condition: Email, Google, GitHub, or LinkedIn signup
branches.path: Begin the selected identity route; its intermediate verification and convergence are not documented publicly.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The identity route changes, but no route can be reconstructed through the required account and dashboard transition.

branches.condition: The developer prefers the Fireworks or OpenAI-compatible SDK tab.
branches.path: Install the selected SDK and run the published example against the same Serverless endpoint.

friction_gates.description: The signup screen offers four peer identity routes without selecting a default.
Signup states that the user agrees to the Terms of Service and Data Processing Agreement.
The post-selection verification, account result, and authenticated dashboard transition are not documented publicly.

time_to_first_success.value: The Serverless Quickstart says it makes the first call in minutes; the blocked public account route prevents end-to-end verification of that claim.

sources.title: Serverless Quickstart
sections_used: Step 1: Create and export an API key
Step 2: Make your first Serverless API call
